concerns.text: T014 schedules SHA-256 checksumming AFTER filtering (T016), violating FR-010 and Constitution Principle III which mandate checksumming 'downloaded datasets' (raw data) to ensure data hygiene. Checksumming filtered data fails to verify the integrity of the raw source artifact.

T031 defers the number of simulated datasets to '[deferred: research decision]', but US-4 Acceptance Scenario 1 and SC-004 explicitly require 'at least 1000 simulated datasets' to validate the ±0.02 CI half-width. This task silently relaxes a hard acceptance criterion.

T043 describes sensitivity analysis sweeping 'low-magnitude values' without specifying the required range {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} defined in Spec Assumptions and FR-008. This creates a risk of scope deviation and fails to satisfy the explicit requirement for the specific alpha sweep.

US-1 Acceptance Scenario 2 & 3 require unit-level verification of Shapiro-Wilk filtering (p < 0.05) and sample size (N ≥ 30) logic. T010a/b only test URL validation, leaving the critical filtering logic untested at the unit level before integration, violating the 'Independent Test' requirement for US-1.
